_meta.notes: Subverted-default race. The genre says halflings are 'small, peace-loving, food-obsessed agrarian villagers'. Here, halflings are wiry naval mercenaries living on coral reefs miles offshore. They eat once a day at dawn. Their freelance navy is feared by every larger fleet on the coast. The Coral Anchorage is anchored to the reef itself; the halflings know which coral is alive and never cut into it.

physicalNotes: Average height 4'1". Wiry, dense muscle, salt-cured skin, calloused feet (most go barefoot — easier on slick decks). Distinctive squint from sun off water.

racialFeatures.label: One meal at dawn
racialFeatures.description: Halflings eat once a day, at first light, and never again until next dawn. The meal is enormous and shared. They consider midday eating a sign of weakness; foreigners who snack in front of them are quietly judged.

racialFeatures.label: Reef-instinct
racialFeatures.description: Adult halflings can read coral by touch and tell living from dead at a glance. They navigate the inner reef in fog without instruments. The Anchorage is built only on dead coral; cutting living coral is a capital offense.

racialFeatures.label: Freelance navy doctrine
racialFeatures.description: The halfling navy is the only standing military among the five races and explicitly hires out — to merchant fleets, to coastal lords, to whoever pays the contract fee. They are feared less for size than for absolute discipline; halfling crews have never broken under fire.

subPopulations.label: Tidehands
subPopulations.summary: The regular standing navy. Six ships, all small and fast. The flagship is the Coral Anchor, four ribbons.

subPopulations.label: Reef-cutters
subPopulations.summary: Privateer crews. Three to five sailors per boat, hired short-term. Notorious for finishing a contract early and asking for the rest of the fee anyway.

subPopulations.label: Salt-keepers
subPopulations.summary: Provisioners. Pack the dawn meal. Cure fish in coral-salt brine. The only halflings who go inland regularly — to the metropolis to trade.

subPopulations.label: Drowncallers
subPopulations.summary: Search-and-rescue when a ship goes down on the reef — and wreckers when they decide it isn't worth saving. The line between is whose flag the ship was flying.

voice: clipped, salt-cured, full of nautical idiom. Smiles a lot — most of the smiles mean nothing.
